key_insight: ReNIO improves on-policy distillation by upweighting likely negative student trajectories using prefix-level probability ratios, rather than treating all self-generated outputs equally.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical training signal for reasoning LLMs that preserves prefix-only efficiency while boosting performance on math and code tasks.
method: Compare student-to-teacher token probabilities along generated prefixes.
Aggregate pivotal low-ratio tokens into a normalized sample weight.
Use the weight to emphasize likely incorrect trajectories during OPD/OPSD training.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports gains on mathematical reasoning and code generation benchmarks, with relative improvements up to 8.90% for Qwen3-1.7B and 10.00% for R1-Distill-Qwen-7B.
watch_item: Because ReNIO relies on prefix-conditioned probability ratios, its effectiveness may depend on calibration quality and the availability of reliable teacher/student probabilities during training.
paper_title: ReNIO: Reweighting Negative Trajectory Importance for LLM On-Policy Distillation
